entries.shortDescription: Iconic nano schooling fish with blue and red striping; one of the most popular aquarium fish in the world.
Brilliant red-and-blue schooling tetra from the Amazon; red stripe runs full body length unlike neon tetra.
Smallest of the neon/cardinal/green neon trio; iridescent blue-green stripe with minimal red.
entries.notes: Also known as false neon tetra, blue neon tetra.

entries.shortDescription: Tiny orange-red nano tetra from Brazil; peaceful schooling fish for planted aquariums.
Hardy South American tetra with pearly white and black horizontal stripes; bold and interactive.
Silvery-gray tetra with tall black dorsal fin and dark oval marking behind gills.
entries.notes: Also called shadow phantom tetra.

entries.shortDescription: Small translucent-red tetra with vivid red face and fins and a black circular patch behind gills.
Popular schooling tetra with distinctive red nose and black-and-white striped tail; sensitive to water quality.
entries.notes: True rummy-nose. Two closely related species also sold as rummy-nose: Hemigrammus bleheri (firehead/brilliant rummy-nose) and Petitella georgiae (false rummy-nose).

entries.shortDescription: Brilliant rummy-nose tetra with more extensive red coloration on head, extending past gill plate.
entries.notes: Also called brilliant rummy-nose tetra. Commonly sold interchangeably with Hemigrammus rhodostomus.

entries.shortDescription: Third member of the rummy-nose group; middle tail stripe extends onto body distinguishing it from others.
Active, bold 2-inch Brazilian schooling fish; males coppery-orange, females lemon-yellow, all with white-tipped fins.
Hardy, active schooling tetra with striking red upper eye; silver body with black spot at tail base.
entries.notes: Also called monk tetra.

entries.shortDescription: African schooling tetra with iridescent flowy fins in red-orange and blue; males develop elaborate fin extensions.
Classic community fish with pinkish-brown body and distinctive triangular black patch; up to 2 inches.
Brighter orange variant of the harlequin rasbora group; slightly smaller with narrower black marking.
entries.notes: Also called Espei rasbora.

entries.shortDescription: Smaller and more slender member of the harlequin group with orange-tinged stripe.
Tiny ¾-inch Borneo nano fish with deep red body and black markings; bold for its size.
entries.notes: Also called mosquito rasbora.

entries.shortDescription: Tiny nano rasbora with a single black-and-yellow spot on the side resembling an exclamation mark.
Nano rasbora with pinkish-red body and scattered dark spots.
Vibrant red nano rasbora from Borneo with prominent black mid-body spot.
Nano fish with vivid lime-green fluorescent stripe; excellent for planted nano tanks.
entries.notes: Also called kubotai rasbora or neon green rasbora.

entries.shortDescription: Slender silver rasbora with forked tail bearing yellow, black, and white banding; active open-water swimmer.
entries.notes: Also called three-lined rasbora.

entries.shortDescription: Elegant nano fish with red snout and caudal fin tips; endemic to Inle Lake, Myanmar.
Larger rasbora with pinkish body and two bold black spots; reaches about 4 inches.
Slender rasbora with a black-edged red stripe running mid-body.
Slim rasbora with a continuous dark lateral stripe running from snout to tail.
Tiny blackwater nano fish with iridescent blue and red coloration; requires soft acidic water.
entries.notes: Also called axelrodi rasbora.

entries.shortDescription: Tiny Myanmar fish with vertical copper-pink bars on a turquoise-green body.
entries.notes: Formerly Celestichthys erythromicron; also called emerald dwarf danio.

entries.shortDescription: Hardy, energetic torpedo-shaped schooling fish with horizontal blue-silver stripes; classic beginner fish.
entries.notes: Leopard danio is the same species (Danio rerio) with spotted pattern. Also sold as GloFish danio in fluorescent color variants. Formerly Brachydanio rerio.

entries.shortDescription: Stunning 1-inch nano fish with golden spots on dark body and brilliant red-orange fins; popular in planted aquascapes.
entries.notes: Also called galaxy rasbora.

entries.shortDescription: Small surface-dwelling danio with orange body and black lateral stripe.
Larger, fast-swimming danio reaching 4 inches with blue and yellow horizontal stripes; classic dither fish.
Peaceful 2-inch Sri Lankan barb; males brilliant cherry-red, females tannish; easy to breed.
Bold 2.5-3 inch barb with four black vertical bars; semi-aggressive schooling fish.
entries.notes: Common color morphs include green tiger barb and albino tiger barb. Also sold as GloFish barb.

entries.shortDescription: Striking 2.5-inch barb with red lateral stripe on males; tolerates cool and tropical temperatures.
Calmer South Asian barb 3-4 inches; males rosy-red, tolerates cooler temps; longfin variants available.
3-inch golden-yellow barb from Vietnam; voracious appetite, entertaining to feed.
Small 1.5-2 inch barb with checkerboard scale pattern; peaceful schooling fish.
entries.notes: Also called checkerboard barb.

entries.shortDescription: Striking 5-inch Indian barb with red horizontal stripe and yellow-and-black tail; also called roseline shark.
entries.notes: Also sold as roseline shark.

entries.shortDescription: Sri Lankan barb; males turn deep ruby-black during breeding; three dark vertical bars.
Delicate-looking Borneo barb with intricate scale patterning resembling snakeskin.
Small Indian barb with pearlescent scales and vertical dark bars.
Most popular aquarium livebearer; males display brilliant colors and flowing fins; prolific breeder.
entries.notes: Hundreds of selectively bred color and fin varieties exist. Also sold as fancy guppy.

entries.shortDescription: Hardy 1-inch nano livebearer; males vibrantly colored; close relative of guppies but less inbred.
Popular livebearer in many colors; peaceful community fish; algae grazer with flat mouth.
entries.notes: Short-fin molly (P. sphenops); sailfin molly (P. latipinna) is a distinct species sold alongside. Many color variants including black molly, dalmatian, lyretail.

entries.shortDescription: Larger livebearer with spectacular tall dorsal fin on males; North American native.
Hardy beginner livebearer from Mexico; 2-3 inches; available in many color varieties.
entries.notes: Southern platy. Variatus platy (Xiphophorus variatus) is a closely related species also commonly sold.

entries.shortDescription: Variable platy with wider range of color patterns than southern platy; tolerates cooler water.
entries.notes: Also called sunset platy, variable platyfish.

entries.shortDescription: Livebearer with elongated lower tail lobe in males; hardy community fish in many colors.
Central American goodeid livebearer; 3.5 inches; less commonly kept conservation species.
1.4-inch West African nano killifish with brown-and-tan vertical bands and flame-colored tail on males.
entries.notes: Also called rocket killifish, banded panchax.

entries.shortDescription: West African killifish with spectacular lyretail; males vivid orange-red with blue spotting.
Hardy West African killifish; males with blue-green iridescence and red spotting.
Hardy top-dwelling Asian killifish; golden body with metallic sheen; effective surface predator.
Florida-native 2-inch pupfish with red and blue horizontal stripes; excellent algae eater for unheated tanks.
entries.notes: Also called Florida flagfish. A North American killifish, not a true killi.

entries.shortDescription: Hardy 1.5-inch Japanese ricefish; excellent for cool or unheated setups; available in many color strains.
entries.notes: Also called Japanese ricefish. Multiple color/fin variants exist (Miyuki blue, white, black, gold, etc.).

entries.shortDescription: Bold 3-inch labyrinth fish; vibrant color variants available; feisty personality.
entries.notes: Color morphs include flame gourami, powder blue gourami, neon blue gourami — all same species.

entries.shortDescription: Peaceful 2-inch labyrinth fish from India and Bangladesh; one of the most beginner-friendly gouramis.
Elegant 5-inch gourami covered in pearl-white spots with a distinctive dark lateral stripe.
Delicate 2.5-inch gourami from Borneo and Sumatra; requires soft, acidic blackwater; mouthbrooder.
Tiny 1.5-inch gourami that produces audible croaking sounds; iridescent spots under lighting.
entries.notes: Also called dwarf croaking gourami, pygmy gourami.

entries.shortDescription: Hardy 2.5-3 inch anabantid with bold red-and-blue striping; tolerates cool water; can be aggressive.
Ambush predator anabantid from Congo with leaf-like camouflage patterning; grows to 6 inches.
entries.notes: Also called Congo leaf fish, spotted ctenopoma.

entries.shortDescription: Iconic Siamese fighting fish; males display spectacular fins; wide variety of fin and color morphs.
entries.notes: Hundreds of selectively bred morphs (halfmoon, crowntail, plakat, dumbo, etc.). GloFish betta also sold.

entries.shortDescription: South American dwarf cichlid with iridescent rainbow coloration; 2-2.5 inches; requires warm water.
entries.notes: Also called blue ram, butterfly cichlid, Ramirez's dwarf cichlid. Electric blue ram is a color morph.

entries.shortDescription: Hardier and more beginner-friendly dwarf cichlid than German blue ram; 3 inches; colorful with red accents.
Most commonly kept Apistogramma; spectacular dorsal fin in males; multiple bred color morphs.
entries.notes: Common name: Cockatoo dwarf cichlid. Color morphs: Super Red, Double Red, Triple Red, Orange Flash.

entries.shortDescription: Popular Apistogramma with paddle-shaped tail; relatively easy to breed; available in Fire Red and Gold forms.
Umbrella dwarf cichlid with light blue body and lemon-yellow fins; tolerates a range of water parameters.
entries.notes: Common name: Umbrella dwarf cichlid.

entries.shortDescription: Three-striped dwarf cichlid with triple black horizontal lines and vibrant blue fins.
entries.notes: Common name: Three-striped dwarf cichlid.

entries.shortDescription: Colombian Apistogramma micropredator with red shoulder markings.
Delicate dwarf cichlid with checkerboard pattern; requires soft, acidic water.
Small South American dwarf cichlid with golden iridescence; females show checkerboard pattern when brooding.
West African dwarf cichlid; females display vivid magenta belly; cave-spawning pair bonder.
entries.notes: Also called purple cichlid, krib.

entries.shortDescription: Iconic laterally-flattened South American cichlid; majestic long fins; 6-8 inches body up to 13 inches tall.
entries.notes: Genus includes P. scalare, P. altum, P. leopoldi. Many color varieties including marble, veil, koi angelfish.

entries.shortDescription: King of the aquarium; 5-7 inch circular South American cichlid with spectacular colors; advanced-level care.
Intelligent, personable 10-12 inch South American cichlid that recognizes owners; long-lived.
entries.notes: Also called water dog. Color morphs include tiger oscar, red oscar, albino oscar.

entries.shortDescription: Hybrid cichlid with rounded body and parrot-like beak; grows to 8 inches; available in multiple colors.
entries.notes: Hybrid species — no valid scientific name. Parent species include Amphilophus citrinellus and Vieja melanurus.

entries.shortDescription: Hybrid New World cichlid with large nuchal hump and vivid pattern; personable but highly territorial.
entries.notes: Hybrid species — no valid scientific name. Derived from various Central American cichlid crosses.

entries.shortDescription: Aggressive 8-10 inch Central American cichlid with iridescent blue-green speckles on dark body.
entries.notes: Electric blue Jack Dempsey is a color morph.

entries.shortDescription: Stunning 6-inch South American cichlid with electric blue iridescence; peaceful relative to many cichlids.
Vividly colored West African cichlid with red body and blue iridescent spots; aggressive breeder.
entries.notes: Genus-level entry; most commonly sold are H. bimaculatus and H. lifalili.

entries.shortDescription: Elegant Tanganyikan cichlid with lyretail and yellow-tipped fins; forms large cooperative family groups.
Brilliant orange-yellow Tanganyikan cichlid; elongated body; rock-dwelling cave spawner.
Elongated Tanganyikan rock cichlid with checkered black-and-white pattern.
entries.notes: Genus Julidochromis includes J. marlieri, J. transcriptus, J. ornatus, J. regani, etc.

entries.shortDescription: World's smallest cichlid; Tanganyikan shell-dwelling species with vertical striping and blue eyes.
entries.notes: Also called multis/multies.

entries.shortDescription: Tanganyikan shell dweller resembling multis but stripes extend all the way to the eyes.
Stockier Tanganyikan shell dweller; gold variety is very colorful; more aggressive than Neolamprologus shell dwellers.
Dwarf mbuna from Lake Malawi; males electric blue, females yellow; peaceful for mbuna.
entries.notes: Formerly Pseudotropheus saulosi.

entries.shortDescription: Most popular and hardy cory species; bronze or albino morph; armored bottom dweller.
entries.notes: Albino cory is the same species (C. aeneus).

entries.shortDescription: Small cory with distinctive panda-like black eye patches and black dorsal and caudal fin markings.
Larger cory with distinctive emerald-green iridescent sheen on upper body.
entries.notes: Also called emerald green cory, green cory, or green brochis. Formerly Brochis splendens.

entries.shortDescription: Cold-tolerant cory with mottled black-and-beige pattern; common and hardy.
Spotted cory with intricate black dots and broken lateral stripe; often confused with C. trilineatus.
Striking cory with white spots on dark body and orange pectoral fins; tolerates higher temperatures.
Tiny 1-inch cory that hovers in midwater; silvery with black horizontal stripe.
Largest of the dwarf cory trio at 1.5 inches; silvery-tan with irregular black stripe; salt-and-pepper cory.
entries.notes: Also called salt and pepper cory. Three dwarf corys: C. habrosus, C. pygmaeus, C. hastatus.

entries.shortDescription: Tiny dwarf cory with a big black spot flanked by white dots at the tail base; midwater swimmer.
Classic algae-eating suckermouth catfish; grows very large (up to 24 inches); not suitable for most home tanks.
entries.notes: Most plecos sold as 'common pleco' may be various Hypostomus or Pterygoplichthys species.

entries.shortDescription: Popular 4-6 inch pleco with bristle-covered snout; prolific algae eater; easy to breed.
entries.notes: Genus-level entry. Multiple Ancistrus species sold as bristlenose; medusa pleco is Ancistrus ranunculus. Longfin, albino, super red variants available.

entries.shortDescription: 4-5 inch pleco with large rubbery lips; excellent algae grazer; prefers cooler water.
entries.notes: Genus-level; commonly C. milesi or C. thomsoni sold as rubber lip pleco.

entries.shortDescription: Small 3.5-inch pleco with bold yellow-and-black banding; wood-eating species; L104.
entries.notes: L-number: L104.

entries.shortDescription: Striking small pleco with bold black-and-white zebra striping; L129.
entries.notes: L-number: L129.

entries.shortDescription: Tiny 1.5-2 inch algae-eating catfish; schooling; peaceful; excellent for planted tanks.
entries.notes: Multiple species sold as otos (O. vittatus, O. macrospilus, O. cocama). O. cocama is zebra oto.

entries.shortDescription: Unique transparent schooling catfish from Thailand; internal organs fully visible through body.
entries.notes: Also called Asian glass catfish, ghost catfish.

entries.shortDescription: African Synodontis that swims habitually upside-down; spots and mottled pattern; 4 inches.
Active 4-5 inch South American catfish with bold black spots, long barbels, and a high-energy personality.
6-inch Southeast Asian algae specialist with a bold black lateral stripe; eats many algae types including black beard.
entries.notes: Formerly C. siamensis. Not to be confused with Chinese algae eater (Gyrinocheilus aymonieri) or flying fox (Epalzeorhynchos kalopterus).

entries.shortDescription: Social Indonesian loach with bright orange-and-black banding; playful personality; grows to 12 inches.
Eel-like 3-4 inch loach with alternating pinkish-yellow and dark brown bands; nocturnal scavenger.
entries.notes: Black kuhli loach (Pangio oblonga) and silver kuhli (P. anguillaris) are distinct but closely related species.

entries.shortDescription: Daytime-active 2-2.5 inch Thai loach with black chain-link pattern; snail eater; swims throughout tank.
entries.notes: Also called pygmy loach.

entries.shortDescription: Stingray-shaped 2.5-inch loach adapted for fast-flowing water; excellent algae grazer.
entries.notes: Also called tiger hillstream loach, gold ring butterfly sucker.

entries.shortDescription: Active 5-6 inch loach with markings that form a 'Y-O-Y-O' pattern; great snail hunter.
entries.notes: Also called Pakistani loach, almora loach.

entries.shortDescription: Beautiful Myanmar loach with bold yellow-and-black polka dot pattern.
entries.notes: Also called polka dot loach.

entries.shortDescription: Indian loach with numerous alternating yellow-and-black vertical stripes; peaceful schooling species.
Tiny Myanmar nano loach; males vivid rosy-orange; one of the smallest loach species.
entries.notes: Undescribed species; sold as Petruichthys sp. 'rosy'.

entries.shortDescription: Elongated, eel-like 12-inch East Asian loach; barometric-pressure sensitive; tolerates cool water.
entries.notes: Also called weather loach, pond loach.

entries.shortDescription: Spectacular Indonesian rainbowfish with iridescent blue front half and orange rear half; 4 inches.
3-inch New Guinea rainbowfish with iridescent blue sheen; males with red-orange fins; beginner-friendly.
entries.notes: Also called praecox rainbowfish, neon rainbowfish.

entries.shortDescription: Vibrant deep-blue and turquoise rainbowfish from Lake Kutubu, Papua New Guinea.
entries.notes: Also called Lake Kutubu rainbowfish, blue rainbowfish.

entries.shortDescription: Large deep-red New Guinea rainbowfish; males brilliant metallic red; grows to 6 inches.
entries.notes: Also called New Guinea red rainbowfish.

entries.shortDescription: Colorful Papua New Guinea rainbowfish with yellow-green sheen and orange fins.
2-inch Papua New Guinea nano rainbowfish with glowing blue eyes, yellow fins, and forked tail.
entries.notes: Also called furcata rainbowfish, forktail blue-eye.

entries.shortDescription: Tiny Papua New Guinea nano rainbowfish with red-orange body and vivid iridescence.
entries.notes: Also called red neon blue-eye.

entries.shortDescription: Delicate nano rainbowfish with elaborate feathery fin extensions on males; requires gentle flow.
entries.notes: Also called featherfin rainbowfish.

entries.shortDescription: Tiny spotted nano rainbowfish with iridescent blue eyes; males with elaborate spotted fins.
entries.notes: Also called spotted blue-eye.

entries.shortDescription: Sulawesi nano rainbowfish with elongated yellow fins and iridescent blue-green body.
South American weakly electric fish with blade-like body; nocturnal; 14-18 inches; uses electrolocation.
Striking black-bodied cyprinid with vivid red tail; territorial bottom-dweller; 5-6 inches.
Gray-bodied Southeast Asian cyprinid with red-orange fins; territorial; 5-6 inches.
entries.notes: Albino variant also sold. Also sold as GloFish rainbow shark.

entries.shortDescription: Silver-bodied active schooling cyprinid with black-edged fins; grows to 12 inches; needs very large tank.
entries.notes: Also called silver shark.

entries.shortDescription: Deep-keeled surface-dwelling South American fish with mottled brown-and-cream pattern; can jump.
Silver-bodied surface-dwelling hatchetfish; strong jumper; must have tight-fitting lid.
entries.notes: Also called silver hatchetfish.

entries.shortDescription: Larger hatchetfish with reflective silver scales; can reach 3 inches.
entries.notes: Also called spotfin hatchetfish.

entries.shortDescription: Smallest hatchetfish species at around 1 inch; surface-dweller.
Unusual pencilfish that swims at a 45-degree angle; slender body with brown striping.
entries.notes: Also called brown pencilfish, diptail pencilfish.

entries.shortDescription: Colorful pencilfish in golden and red forms; midwater swimmer.
Vivid red-bodied pencilfish; one of the most colorful species in the genus; requires live plants.
entries.notes: Also called red arc pencilfish.

entries.shortDescription: Surface-dwelling pencilfish with three distinct longitudinal stripes.
West African weakly electric fish with prominent trunk-like appendage; 9 inches; highly intelligent.
Surface-dwelling West African predator with wing-like pectoral fins; excellent jumper; 5 inches.
Surface-dwelling livebearer from Sulawesi with elongated lower jaw; males with colorful fin markings.
World's smallest pufferfish at 1 inch; South Indian freshwater; helicopter-like movement; curious personality.
entries.notes: Also called Indian dwarf puffer, Malabar puffer, pygmy puffer.

entries.shortDescription: 3-inch community-compatible freshwater puffer with golden-banded pattern; long-lived at 8-10+ years.
entries.notes: Also called South American puffer (SAP).

entries.shortDescription: Largest freshwater puffer at 22-30 inches; highly intelligent; recognizes owners; requires massive tank.
Hardy 1.5-inch Chinese minnow with white horizontal stripe and red accents; thrives in cool unheated tanks.
entries.notes: Gold and longfin variants available.

entries.shortDescription: Selectively bred egg-shaped double-tailed goldfish including orandas, ranchus, ryukins, and more.
entries.notes: Fancy goldfish is a group of breeds; all are Carassius auratus. Varieties include oranda, ranchu, ryukin, telescope, butterfly, lion head, bubble eye, pearlscale, etc.

entries.shortDescription: Single-tailed streamlined goldfish; hardiest goldfish type; comet and common variants.
entries.notes: Same species as fancy goldfish (C. auratus). Includes comet, common, shubunkin varieties.

entries.shortDescription: Fully aquatic dwarf frog sold in the fish trade; 1.5 inches; bottom scavenger; social.
entries.notes: Scientific name not provided in AC article. Commonly Hymenochirus boettgeri (or H. curtipes). NOT African clawed frog (Xenopus laevis).

entries.shortDescription: Hardy South American tetra with black-and-silver body and dramatic flowing black skirt fins.
entries.notes: Also called black widow tetra. Also sold as GloFish tetra in fluorescent color variants.
